notes: Lesson 18 Part 2 (act 105), 'Same-direction read/write', 89.3s, 6 cues. In-place compaction (remove duplicates from a sorted array [1,1,2,3,3]): both pointers move the same direction — read scans every element, write marks the boundary of the compacted prefix. write only advances (and copies) when read finds a value different from the last kept one, so the unique prefix [1,2,3] is built in place with O(n) work and O(1) space. Trace: write=1 seeded (first element trivially kept) -> read's second 1 is a duplicate, skip (write stays) -> read's 2 is fresh, copy to the write slot, advance write -> read n + write<=n, neither backs up -> 2n=O(n); the bug: advancing write blindly copies duplicates back in (corrupted prefix [1,1,2]); answer = prefix [1,2,3] before write. read sits ABOVE the row (side UP), write BELOW (side DOWN) so the two same-direction pointers never collide. arrays.py idioms; MathTex reserved for the 2n=O(n) bound. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke, then full -ql render + READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues. Iteration 2 fixed cue01 and cue02, where an above-row caption collided with the read pointer's label (side UP owns the space above the row) — moved both captions below the row and re-rendered + re-verified clean. Per-cue checklist (legibility + correctness, highlight matches beat, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping, timing tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 89.201s vs audio 89.304s (delta 0.103s, within tolerance).
